author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

metadata.author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon

title: Canon X.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: It shall not be lawful for a clergyman to be at the same time enrolled in the churches of two cities, that is, in the church in which he was at first ordained, and in another to which, because it is greater, he has removed from lust of empty honour.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And those who do so shall be returned to their own church in which they were originally ordained, and there only shall they minister.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if any one has heretofore been removed from one church to another, he shall not intermeddle with the affairs of his former church, nor with the martyries, almshouses, and hostels belonging to it.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And if, after the decree of this great and ecumenical Synod, any shall dare to do any of these things now forbidden, the synod decrees that he shall be degraded from his rank.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon X.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: No cleric shall be recorded on the clergy-list of the churches of two cities.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if he shall have strayed forth, let him be returned to his former place.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if he has been transferred, let him have no share in the affairs of his former church.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Van Espen, following Christian Lupus, remarks that this canon is opposed to pluralities.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: For if a clergyman has by presentation and institution obtained two churches, he is enrolled in two churches at the same time, contrary to this canon; but surely that this be the case, the two churches must needs be in two cities, and that, in the days of Chalcedon, meant in two dioceses.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Here a new institution comes into view, of which there were many instances.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Julian had directed Pagan hospices (ξενοδοχεῖα ) to be established on the Christian model (Epist. xlix.).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The Basiliad at Cæsarea was a ξενοδοχεῖον as well as a πτωχεῖον; it contained καταγώγια τοῖς ξένοις, as well as for wayfayers, and those who needed assistance on account of illness, and Basil distinguished various classes of persons engaged in charitable ministrations, including those who escorted the traveller on his way (τοὺς παραπέμποντας , Epist. xciv.).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Jerome writes to Pammachius: “I hear that you have made a ‘xenodochion’ in the port of Rome,” and adds that he himself had built a “diversorium” for pilgrims to Bethlehem (Epist. xvi., 11, 14).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Chrysostom reminds his auditors at Constantinople that “there is a common dwelling set apart by the Church,” and “called a xenon” (in Act.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: His friend Olympias was munificent to “xenotrophia” (Hist.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Lausiac, 144).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: There was a xenodochion near the church of the monastic settlement at Nitria (ib., 7).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ischyrion, in his memorial read in the 3d session of Chalcedon, complains of his patriarch Dioscorus for having misapplied funds bequeathed by a charitable lady τοῖς ξενεῶσι καὶ πτωχείοις in Egypt, and says that he himself had been confined by Dioscorus in a “xenon” for lepers (Mansi, vi.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Justinian mentions xenodochia in Cod., i.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: 3, 49, and their wardens in Novell., 134, 16.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Gregory the Great orders that the accounts of xenodochia should be audited by the bishop (Epist. iv., 27).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Charles the Great provides for the restoration of decayed “senodochia” (Capitul. of 803; Pertz, Leg., i.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: 110); and Alcuin exhorts his pupil, archbishop Eanbald, to think where in the diocese of York he could establish “xenodochia, id est, hospitalia” (Epist.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars II., Causa XXI., Q.
